source_book: 《不拘一格》 里德·哈斯廷斯 / 艾琳·迈耶

test_cases.prompt: We want to implement 360-degree reviews at our company. How do we design them so they actually lead to improvement instead of being a checkbox exercise?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should trigger the feedback-loop-360 skill and explain how to implement 360 reviews that drive real behavioral change, drawing from Netflix's approach to structured team feedback.

test_cases.prompt: I think anonymous 360 feedback is useless — people either sugarcoat or use it to settle scores. Is there a better way to do this?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should trigger the feedback-loop-360 skill and address the anonymity problem, explaining why signed, transparent 360 feedback is more effective than anonymous approaches.

test_cases.prompt: How do I set up a structured team feedback process where people actually give each other honest, actionable input on a regular basis?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should trigger the feedback-loop-360 skill and provide a framework for building a regular, structured feedback loop within a team.

test_cases.prompt: What's a good daily standup format? We're an agile team and our standups have become unfocused and too long.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should NOT trigger the feedback-loop-360 skill. This is about meeting facilitation and agile process, not about peer feedback systems or 360-degree reviews.

test_cases.prompt: Can you give me a one-on-one meeting template? I have weekly 1:1s with my direct reports and want to make them more productive.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should NOT trigger the feedback-loop-360 skill. This is about manager-report meeting structure, not about multi-directional peer feedback or 360 review systems.

test_cases.prompt: My startup team is only 3 people. Is implementing a formal 360 feedback process overkill for such a small team? Or is there value in starting the habit early?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should trigger the skill but with adaptation guidance. A 3-person team makes formal 360 reviews feel excessive, but the underlying principles (regular, structured, signed feedback) can be scaled down. The skill should provide a lightweight version appropriate for small teams while preserving the core philosophy.

notes: Test cases for the feedback-loop-360 concept from No Rules Rules. Netflix's 360 process emphasizes signed (non-anonymous) feedback, actionable input, and regular cadence. The skill should help teams design and implement 360 systems that produce honest improvement, not just paperwork. Small-team edge cases should be handled with a simplified adaptation.
